ui.manual.title: Manual
ui.manual.tab.cards.title: Cards
ui.manual.tab.battle.title: Battle
ui.manual.tab.map.title: Map
ui.manual.gameplay.overview.title: Overview
ui.manual.gameplay.overview.body: Battle is a turn-based confrontation between your units and the enemy. Each turn is split into two phases: the Assignment Phase, where you prepare your cards and targets, and the Combat Phase, where all assigned cards resolve in speed order.
ui.manual.battle.turn.title: Turn Structure
ui.manual.battle.turn.step1.title: Turn Start
ui.manual.battle.turn.step1.body: Each unit's card slots are refreshed and assigned new speed values, randomly rolled from that unit's speed range. On the first turn each unit draws 4 cards, and every subsequent turn they draw 1 card. All units restore 1 MP by default. Turn start effects trigger.
ui.manual.battle.turn.step2.title: Assignment Phase
ui.manual.battle.turn.step2.body: Select a card slot above one of your units, then choose a card from their hand to assign to it. Finally, click a valid target's card slot to set who that card will act against. Repeat for all units you wish to act this turn.
ui.manual.battle.turn.step3.title: Combat Phase
ui.manual.battle.turn.step3.body: All assigned cards resolve in order from highest speed to lowest. Each card triggers its effects and actions in sequence (top to bottom) until all have been played.
ui.manual.battle.turn.step4.title: Turn End
ui.manual.battle.turn.step4.body: Once all cards have resolved and any end-of-turn effects have triggered, the next turn begins.
ui.manual.gameplay.winning.title: Victory & Defeat
ui.manual.gameplay.winning.body: You win the battle when all enemy units are reduced to zero health. You lose if all of your units are defeated. Some encounters have additional win or loss conditions - read the encounter description carefully before engaging.
ui.manual.cards.anatomy.title: Anatomy of a Card
ui.manual.cards.anatomy.body: Each card has several key properties. Understanding them will help you plan your assignments effectively.
ui.manual.cards.anatomy.name: Name
ui.manual.cards.actions.title: Card Actions
ui.manual.cards.actions.attack.name: Attack
ui.manual.cards.actions.attack.desc: Deals physical damage to a target. The amount of damage is determined by the action's power and any relevant effect modifiers.
ui.manual.cards.actions.defense.name: Defense
ui.manual.cards.actions.defense.desc: Provides shield for a unit. The amount of shield gained is determined by the action's power and any relevant effect modifiers.
ui.manual.cards.types.title: Card Types
ui.manual.cards.types.attack.name: Attack
ui.manual.cards.types.attack.desc: Deals damage to a target. Attack cards are the primary source of damage and often carry secondary effects such as applying status effects.
ui.manual.cards.types.defense.name: Defense
ui.manual.cards.types.defense.desc: Provides protection for a unit. Defense cards may reduce incoming damage, apply shields, restore health, or otherwise prevent your units from taking harm.
ui.manual.cards.types.spell.name: Spell
ui.manual.cards.types.spell.desc: Triggers effects. Spells can heal allies, buff stats, generate MP, manipulate speed, or apply powerful status effects.
ui.manual.cards.types.item.name: Item
ui.manual.cards.types.item.desc:
Consumable cards that provide immediate utility such as restoring health or MP, granting buffs, or empowering other cards.
Item cards are expended upon play and removed from the deck.
ui.manual.battle.declaring.title: Speed & Order
ui.manual.battle.declaring.body: Each card slot's speed is randomly rolled at the start of every turn from that unit's speed range. If two slots share the same speed, one is chosen randomly to go first. Plan your assignments with this uncertainty in mind.
ui.manual.battle.death.title: Unit Death
ui.manual.battle.death.body: When a unit's health reaches zero, they are removed from battle immediately. Any card slots they had assigned are cancelled. If one of your units falls mid-combat, their remaining assignments for that turn are lost. If you die, the run is lost. If your allies die, they perish. Defeat your enemies to win the battle.
ui.manual.battle.status.title: Status Effects
ui.manual.battle.status.body: Status effects are conditions applied to units that modify their stats, actions, or interactions during battle. They can be applied by cards, relics or passive abilities and persist until their duration expires or a trigger removes them.
ui.manual.battle.shield.title: Shield & On-Hit Effects
ui.manual.battle.shield.body: Shield absorbs incoming damage before it reaches a unit's health. If a hit is fully absorbed by shield, it is not considered a hit — on-hit effects will not trigger. Only damage that reaches health counts as a real hit.
ui.manual.map.overview.title: The Map
ui.manual.map.overview.body: The map represents your journey through the world. From your current position you can follow one or more paths forward, each leading to a different node. Choose your route carefully — you can only visit nodes along connected paths and cannot backtrack.
ui.manual.map.nodes.title: Node Types
ui.manual.map.nodes.body: Each node on the map represents a different kind of encounter or opportunity. Learn what each one offers so you can plan your path accordingly.
ui.manual.map.nodes.battle.name: Battle
ui.manual.map.nodes.battle.desc: A standard enemy encounter. Defeating all enemies rewards you with gold and a chance to add a card to your storage.
ui.manual.map.nodes.elite.name: Elite
ui.manual.map.nodes.elite.desc: A tougher enemy encounter with stronger foes. Rewards are greater, but so is the risk. Defeating an Elite battle awards a relic choice. Approach only when you are well-prepared.
ui.manual.map.nodes.event.name: Event
ui.manual.map.nodes.event.desc: A narrative encounter that usually presents a choice. Outcomes vary - some are beneficial, some carry risk. Read carefully before committing.
ui.manual.map.nodes.rest.name: Rest Site
ui.manual.map.nodes.rest.desc: A safe haven where you and your party will recover.
ui.manual.map.nodes.shop.name: Shop
ui.manual.map.nodes.shop.desc: A merchant offering packs of cards, relics and healing in exchange for gold. Revisit your strategy here.
ui.manual.map.nodes.boss.name: Boss
ui.manual.map.nodes.boss.desc: A powerful enemy that marks the end of a floor. Defeating the boss unlocks the next floor and awards a powerful relic. Upon moving to the next floor, all your units' health and MP are fully restored.
ui.manual.map.paths.title: Paths & Progression
ui.manual.map.paths.body: At each node you will see one or more highlighted paths leading to the next row. You may only travel along connected paths - not every node on the map is reachable in a single run. Each floor culminates in a Boss node. Clearing it advances you to the next floor with a new map layout.
ui.manual.map.tip.title: Tip
ui.manual.map.tip.body: Plan several nodes ahead rather than one at a time. Favor Rest Sites before Boss encounters and Shops after long battle chains when your gold is high.
ui.manual.tab.relics.title: Relics
ui.manual.relics.overview.title: Relics
ui.manual.relics.overview.body: Relics are passive items that persist for the entire run. Each relic provides a unique effect that modifies your units, cards, or battle rules in some way. Some relics are always active, while others must be equipped for their effects to apply. You may equip or unequip relics in the team edit screen before battles. Certain relics may have drawbacks.
ui.manual.relics.obtaining.elite.name: Elite Battles
ui.manual.relics.obtaining.elite.desc: Defeating an Elite battle always awards a relic choice.
ui.manual.relics.obtaining.boss.name: Boss Battles
ui.manual.relics.obtaining.boss.desc: Defeating a Boss battle awards a powerful relic choice.
ui.manual.relics.obtaining.shop.name: Shop
ui.manual.relics.obtaining.shop.desc: Relics appear in the shop as you progress, offering a chance to purchase them with gold. Shop relics are randomly selected from the pool of relics available on that floor, which may include common, uncommon, rare and legendary relics.
ui.manual.relics.obtaining.start.name: Starting Relics
ui.manual.relics.obtaining.start.desc: At the start of a run you may choose one of several common relics to begin with. These relics are randomly selected from the pool of common relics available on the first floor.
ui.manual.relics.obtaining.trading.name: Relic Trading
ui.manual.relics.obtaining.trading.desc: In the shop, you may trade 2 relics from your current collection of the same rarity to receive a new random relic of the next higher rarity. Certain relic combinations may yield specific results. Experiment with different trades to discover powerful synergies and unique relics.
ui.manual.relics.obtaining.event.name: Events
ui.manual.relics.obtaining.event.desc: Some narrative event nodes may grant relics as an outcome, sometimes at a cost or risk.
ui.manual.relics.tiers.title: Relic Tiers
ui.manual.relics.tiers.body: Relics come in four tiers that indicate their general power level.
ui.manual.relics.tiers.common.name: Common
ui.manual.relics.tiers.common.desc: Straightforward effects that usually provide a reliable bonus. May come with drawbacks. These form the backbone of most builds. Certain common relics are available to be chosen at the start of the run.
ui.manual.relics.tiers.uncommon.name: Uncommon
ui.manual.relics.tiers.uncommon.desc: More complex effects that can enable specific strategies or combos. Found less frequently than common relics.
ui.manual.relics.tiers.rare.name: Rare
ui.manual.relics.tiers.rare.desc: More impactful effects that can meaningfully shift your strategy. Found less frequently.
ui.manual.relics.tiers.legendary.name: Legendary
ui.manual.relics.tiers.legendary.desc: The most powerful relics, offering unique and game-changing effects. Extremely rare.
ui.manual.relics.tip.title: Tip
ui.manual.relics.tip.body: Relics compound over a run — a relic that seems minor early on can become decisive once you have several cards built around it. Keep future synergies in mind when making your choice.
